channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok Ban: U.S. Supreme Court Upholds Law Haltin App's Downloads
item.description: The U.S. is inching closer to a potential TikTok ban, with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok's Demise: A Blow to Free Expression and a Wake-Up Call for Politicians
item.description: The Supreme Court's decision to uphold the ban on TikTok has sent shockwaves through the tech world, leaving 170 million users in limbo. The implications are far-reaching, affecting not only the app but also the young people who call it their home.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do
item.description: Robin Carnahan, the out-going GSA administrator, said the federal footprint shrunk by 11 million square feet and saved $2 billion in 2024 alone. The post Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk’s Starship rocket explodes during SpaceX flight test
item.description: A SpaceX Starship rocket broke up in space minutes after launching from Texas on Thursday, forcing airline flights over the Gulf of Mexico to alter course to avoid falling debris and setting back Elon Musk’s flagship rocket program.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 59 organizations reportedly victim to breaches caused by Cleo software bug
item.description: Unsurprisingly, spokespeople for Clop’s corporate victims are being coy about the extent of the intrusions into their servers, or refusing to admit any at all.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Gorsuch warns foreign adversaries could replace TikTok for surveillance
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Neil Gorsuch expressed concerns Friday that the divest-or-ban law targeting TikTok might not "achieve its goals." Gorsuch warned that foreign adversaries could ultimately turn to another application for surveillance of U.S. data if TikTok is no longer used in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: White House says TikTok ban will fall to Trump administration
item.description: The White House said Friday that the TikTok ban will fall to President-elect Trump's administration after the Supreme Court upheld a law requiring the app's China-based parent company to divest from it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: TikTok ban upheld by Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok and ByteDance — along with some content creators and users — argued the law violates constitutionally protected free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court upholds TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld a law requiring TikTok’s China-based parent company to divest from the app, teeing up a ban set to take effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Best ways to give your old iPhone a second life
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the many different ways you can repurpose that old iPhone of yours rather than trashing it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Python eats the world
item.description: In this week’s Python Report: Everyone’s favorite snake-named language is getting closer to being everyone’s favorite language, period. We also have a guide to unpacking the power of Python’s abstract base classes, and a tutorial for developers who want to use Cython to speed up their Python code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok refugees or propagandists? China’s RedNote users share mixed responses to U.S. influx
item.description: Users of the Chinese social media app RedNote welcomed “TikTok refugees” from the United States with selfies and messages on Wednesday, as Beijing said it encouraged stronger cultural ties with other countries in response to the sudden influx.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Google inks a Gemini AI chatbot deal with the Associated Press
item.description: Google says its artificial intelligence chatbot Gemini will deliver up-to-date news from the Associated Press in the tech giant’s first such deal with a news publisher.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: TikTok users are flocking to alternative apps like RedNote
item.description: TikTok is still three days away from a likely ban in the United States, but many users are already bidding the app farewell and seeking out alternatives.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Kazakhstan’s AI-Powered Prisons: Setting a New Standard for Safety and Efficiency
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI health tech will transform your fitness routine
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US TikTok users flock to Chinese app Xiaohongshu in protest with TikTok ban looming
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — As the threat of a TikTok ban looms, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ericsson unveils genAI assistant for 5G network operations
item.description: Telecommunications and networking provider Ericsson recently launched its generative AI-based virtual assistant that uses large language model (LLM) technology to read, understand, and generate new content to provide personalized answers for network operators configuring wireless 5G networks, troubl
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Polish town invites Musk to buy castle for his European HQ
item.description: Tech billionaire is reportedly looking for a sizeable location, possibly a castle in Italy, as a hub for his operations in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The cloud cost wake-up call I predicted
item.description: For years, I’ve cautioned organizations about the hidden downsides of cloud computing. Ironically, I’m “Dave the cloud guy” who warns enterprises about the cloud. The benefits of the cloud’s agility, scalability, and innovation can quickly become a financial sinkhole without proper management.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Yes, you should use AI coding assistants—but not like that
item.description: If you’re exhausted by the constantly changing AI landscape, you’re not alone. In a thoughtful post, Microsoft Research brainiac Victor Dibia captures the “particular kind of fatigue that comes from trying to match the unprecedented pace of AI advancement.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path
item.description: CISA’s National Initiative for Cybersecurity Careers and Studies helps cyber pros keep up with technology changes ranging from AI to zero trust. The post CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Best cutting-edge health and fitness tech from CES 2025
item.description: CES 2025 showcased everything from artificial intelligence-powered sleep aids to smart ear-cleaning devices. Kurt the CyberGuy has the details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
